text: **Anna:** This is my {driver's license|運転免許証|/ˈdraɪvərz ˈlaɪsəns/|I need my driver's license to drive.}!
**Anna:** I passed my {driving test|運転試験|/ˈdraɪvɪŋ test/|I studied hard for my driving test.}
**Anna:** Today I {rented|借りた|/rent/|I rented a bike for the day.} a car so I can drive around {Washington DC|アメリカの首都|/ˈwɑʃɪŋtən diˈsi/|Washington DC is the capital of the USA.}
**Anna:** You can see more of the city this way.
**Marsha:** Did you get your driver's license?
**Anna:** I did but it was not {easy|簡単/容易|/ˈizi/|Math is not easy for me.}
**Anna:** Well, you know, I can drive {farm equipment|農業機械|/fɑːrm ɪˈkwɪpmənt/|Farm equipment helps farmers work faster.} really well.
**Anna:** I was really {nervous|緊張した/不安な|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|I feel nervous before a test.} driving in Washington DC {traffic|交通/交通量|/ˈtræfɪk/|Traffic is heavy during rush hour.}
**Marsha:** Did you pass the test the first time?
**Anna:** But I did pass the second time.
**Marsha:** What happened during the first test?
**John:** Okay, Anna, is your {seatbelt|シートベルト|/ˈsiːtbelt/|Always wear your seatbelt.} {buckled|締められた|/ˈbʌkld/|My seatbelt is buckled.}?
**John:** Why are you talking to yourself?
**Anna:** I am a little {nervous|緊張した/不安な|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|I feel nervous before a test.}
**Anna:** When I'm nervous, I talk to myself.
**John:** You don't need to be {nervous|緊張する/不安な|/ˈnɜːrvəs/|I feel nervous before exams.}
**Anna:** Listen to that {engine|エンジン/機関|/ˈendʒɪn/|The car engine is loud.}
**Anna:** Please, stop {pushing|押す/踏む|/ˈpʊʃɪŋ/|Stop pushing the button.} the {gas pedal|アクセル|/ɡæs ˈpɛdl/|Press the gas pedal slowly.}
**John:** Okay, when you are ready, {turn|曲がる/方向転換する|/tɜːrn/|Turn left at the corner.}
**Anna:** You almost {hit|当たる/衝突する|/hɪt/|Be careful not to hit the wall.} that car.
**John:** There were cars in the {street|通り/道路|/striːt/|Walk on the left side of the street.}
**Anna:** Don't {yell|叫ぶ/大声で言う|/jɛl/|Don't yell at me.} at me.
**Anna:** I was {afraid|恐れている/怖い|/əˈfreɪd/|I am afraid of dogs.}
**Anna:** {Break|ブレーキ/止める|/breɪk/|Break the car quickly.}, break.
**Anna:** Why is everyone {honking|クラクションを鳴らす|/ˈhɒŋkɪŋ/|Stop honking at me.} at us?
**John:** You are driving too {slow|遅い/ゆっくり|/sloʊ/|Drive slow in the school zone.}
**John:** Hands on the {wheel|ハンドル/方向盤|/wiːl/|Keep your hands on the wheel.}, Anna.
**Anna:** Yes, it did not go well.
**Anna:** But I {practiced|練習した|/ˈpræktɪst/|I practiced piano every day.} and passed the second time.
**Marsha:** Do you know where you want to take your first {drive|運転/ドライブ|/draɪv/|Let's go for a drive.} in Washington DC?
**Anna:** The {White House|大統領官邸|/waɪt haʊs/|The White House is in Washington, D.C.}!
**Marsha:** You do know you can't {drive up|近づく|/draɪv ʌp/|We can't drive up to the building.} to the White House, don't you?
**Anna:** I {guess|思う/推測する|/ɡes/|I guess it will rain today.} we walk from here.
**Anna:** Sometimes you can see more of {Washington|ワシントン|/ˈwɑːʃɪŋtən/|Washington is the capital of the U.S.} D.
**Anna:** If you want to see the White House, you need to {walk|歩く/散歩する|/wɔːk/|Let's walk in the park.}
